malware-analyst.title: Foundations of Malware Analysis
malware-analyst.description: Build foundational knowledge of how malware operates at the OS and CPU level. Understand memory, processes, and file system concepts before touching any sample.
topics: Setting up an isolated analysis VM
malware-analyst.challenge: Set up a Windows 10 analysis VM with snapshots and document the PE structure of a benign executable using a hex editor.

malware-analyst.title: Static Analysis Techniques
malware-analyst.description: Examine malware without executing it by extracting strings, imports, and metadata from binary files. Learn to write YARA rules to classify samples by pattern.
topics: Using VirusTotal and threat intel feeds
malware-analyst.challenge: Write three YARA rules that detect different malware families based on string and import patterns from public samples.

malware-analyst.title: Dynamic Analysis and Sandboxing
malware-analyst.description: Execute malware in a controlled environment and observe its behavior in real time. Capture registry changes, network traffic, and file system modifications.
topics: Monitoring file system and registry changes
Using Cuckoo Sandbox for automated reports
malware-analyst.challenge: Analyze a known ransomware sample in Cuckoo and produce a full behavioral report listing all file, registry, and network indicators.

malware-analyst.title: Disassembly with Ghidra
malware-analyst.description: Use Ghidra to decompile and annotate malware binaries to understand code logic. Practice renaming variables, functions, and structures to build readable pseudo-code.
topics: Ghidra project setup and binary import
Navigating the disassembly and decompiler views
malware-analyst.challenge: Reverse engineer a crackme binary in Ghidra, identify the correct serial key logic, and document each function with descriptive names.

malware-analyst.title: Anti-Analysis Techniques
malware-analyst.description: Understand the methods malware uses to detect and evade analysis tools, debuggers, and sandboxes. Learn countermeasures to defeat anti-debugging, anti-VM, and packing tricks.
topics: Defeating anti-analysis with patches and plugins
malware-analyst.challenge: Manually unpack a UPX-packed sample and a custom-packed sample, restoring the original IAT and producing a runnable unpacked binary.

malware-analyst.title: Rootkits and Kernel-Mode Malware
malware-analyst.description: Investigate malware that operates at the kernel level to hide processes, files, and network connections. Learn to use kernel debuggers and live memory analysis to expose rootkit behavior.
topics: Detecting hidden processes with memory analysis
malware-analyst.challenge: Analyze a publicly available rootkit sample in a kernel debugging session and identify all SSDT hooks and hidden objects it installs.

malware-analyst.title: Scripting and Automation for Analysis
malware-analyst.description: Automate repetitive analysis tasks using Python and Ghidra scripts to scale sample processing. Build tooling to extract IOCs, rename functions, and classify samples in bulk.
topics: Python pefile library for PE parsing
malware-analyst.challenge: Write a Python script that accepts a directory of PE samples, extracts all imports and strings, scores them with YARA, and outputs a CSV report.

malware-analyst.title: Malware Families and Campaign Analysis
malware-analyst.description: Study real-world malware families - ransomware, RATs, banking trojans, and wipers - to understand campaign tactics and threat actor patterns. Map observed behaviors to MITRE ATT&CK techniques.
topics: Using open CTI platforms and feeds
malware-analyst.challenge: Reverse engineer a recent ransomware sample, map all observed behaviors to ATT&CK, and write a two-page threat intelligence report.

malware-analyst.title: Memory Forensics and Advanced Evasion
malware-analyst.description: Analyze in-memory malware that leaves minimal disk artifacts and uses advanced injection techniques. Use Volatility to extract and reconstruct malicious code from memory dumps.
malware-analyst.challenge: Obtain a memory image containing a fileless implant, use Volatility to locate and extract the injected shellcode, and analyze it in Ghidra.

malware-analyst.title: Production Malware Analysis Operations
malware-analyst.description: Operate as a professional malware analyst inside a SOC or threat intelligence team, managing sample intake, detection development, and intelligence sharing. Build and maintain an automated analysis pipeline integrated with threat intel platforms.
topics: Designing a malware analysis lab at scale
Integrating CAPE with MISP and OpenCTI
Detection rule development from analysis output
Sample triage and priority queue management
Metrics and KPIs for analysis programs
malware-analyst.challenge: Build an end-to-end pipeline that ingests samples from email, detonates them in CAPE, pushes IOCs to MISP, and auto-generates Sigma detection rules.

grc-analyst.title: Introduction to GRC and Compliance
grc-analyst.description: Understand the three pillars of GRC - Governance, Risk, and Compliance - and why organizations invest in structured programs. Learn how regulations and voluntary frameworks shape security programs.
topics: What is GRC and why it matters
Overview of major frameworks (ISO, NIST, SOC 2)
GRC analyst career paths and responsibilities
grc-analyst.challenge: Research one real-world data breach, identify which compliance failures contributed to it, and map them to a specific framework control.

grc-analyst.title: NIST Cybersecurity Framework
grc-analyst.description: Deep-dive into the NIST Cybersecurity Framework and its five core functions - Identify, Protect, Detect, Respond, Recover. Apply the framework to assess an organization against target maturity profiles.
topics: Creating a Current and Target Profile
grc-analyst.challenge: Perform a CSF gap assessment on a fictional mid-size company scenario and produce a prioritized remediation roadmap for the top 10 control gaps.

grc-analyst.title: ISO 27001 Information Security Management
grc-analyst.description: Understand ISO 27001 as an internationally recognized ISMS standard and what achieving certification requires. Learn to scope an ISMS, perform gap analysis, and prepare documentation for certification audits.
topics: Risk assessment and Statement of Applicability
Annex A control selection and justification
Internal audit process and corrective actions
grc-analyst.challenge: Write a draft Statement of Applicability for a fictional SaaS company, justifying inclusion or exclusion of all Annex A controls with risk rationale.

grc-analyst.title: SOC 2 Trust Services Criteria
grc-analyst.description: Learn the AICPA Trust Services Criteria that form the basis of SOC 2 reports and how service organizations demonstrate control effectiveness. Understand the difference between Type I point-in-time and Type II over-time engagements.
topics: Trust Services Criteria (Security, Availability, Confidentiality, Privacy, Processing Integrity)
SOC 2 Type I vs Type II differences
grc-analyst.challenge: Map 20 sample security controls to the relevant Trust Services Criteria categories and draft evidence descriptions for an auditor evidence request list.

grc-analyst.title: Risk Management and Risk Registers
grc-analyst.description: Build and maintain risk registers using both qualitative and quantitative risk methodologies to support business decisions. Learn the FAIR model for translating cyber risk into financial terms executives understand.
topics: Introduction to FAIR quantitative risk analysis
Building and maintaining a risk register
Risk treatment options (accept, mitigate, transfer, avoid)

resources.label: How to Measure Anything in Cybersecurity Risk

grc-analyst.challenge: Build a 25-item risk register for a fictional e-commerce company, score each risk qualitatively, and produce a heat map with recommended treatment decisions.

grc-analyst.title: Policy Development and Management
grc-analyst.description: Develop, maintain, and enforce information security policies that align business requirements with regulatory obligations. Learn the full policy lifecycle from drafting through annual review and exception handling.
topics: Writing clear and enforceable security policies
Managing policy review and approval workflows
grc-analyst.challenge: Draft a complete Acceptable Use Policy and a companion exception request process with an SLA, approval matrix, and risk escalation path.

grc-analyst.title: Third-Party and Vendor Risk Management
grc-analyst.description: Assess and manage the security risks introduced by third-party vendors, suppliers, and partners who access or process organizational data. Build a vendor risk program with tiering, questionnaires, and contractual requirements.
topics: Vendor risk tiering and inherent risk classification
Reviewing vendor SOC 2 reports and certifications
Security requirements in contracts and BAAs

resources.label: Supply Chain Risk Management Guide (NIST SP 800-161)

grc-analyst.challenge: Build a vendor risk program template including a tiering matrix, a 30-question security questionnaire for high-risk vendors, and a remediation tracking SLA table.

grc-analyst.title: Audit Management and Evidence Programs
grc-analyst.description: Manage internal and external audit engagements from scoping through remediation closure, using GRC platforms to automate evidence collection. Learn to write audit findings and track remediation plans.
topics: Writing clear and actionable audit findings
Using Vanta or Drata for continuous compliance

resources.label: Certified Information Systems Auditor (CISA) Study Guide

grc-analyst.challenge: Simulate an audit by drafting a full audit plan, requesting 15 evidence items, writing three sample findings with risk ratings, and creating a CAP for each.

grc-analyst.title: Privacy Regulations and Data Governance
grc-analyst.description: Navigate major privacy regulations and build data governance programs that align compliance obligations with data lifecycle management. Learn to conduct Data Protection Impact Assessments and manage data subject rights requests.
topics: GDPR principles and lawful bases for processing
CCPA and US state privacy law landscape
Data mapping and Records of Processing Activities (RoPA)
grc-analyst.challenge: Create a data mapping inventory for a fictional health-tech startup, perform a DPIA for its primary data processing activity, and identify applicable transfer mechanisms.

grc-analyst.title: Integrated GRC Program Leadership
grc-analyst.description: Lead a mature, integrated GRC program that aligns multiple frameworks under a unified control library while reporting meaningful metrics to executive leadership and the board. Drive continuous compliance automation and culture of accountability.
topics: Unified control framework and control mapping
Building a GRC platform (ServiceNow GRC, Archer)
Developing GRC program metrics and OKRs
Managing regulatory change and horizon scanning
Building a culture of compliance across the organization
grc-analyst.challenge: Design a unified GRC program for a company subject to ISO 27001, SOC 2, and GDPR, producing a single control library, a board risk dashboard, and a 3-year maturity roadmap.

security-architect.title: Security Architecture Fundamentals
security-architect.description: Establish the foundational mindset and vocabulary of security architecture, separating it from engineering and operations roles. Learn classic architecture frameworks and security design principles used throughout a career.
topics: Role of a security architect vs engineer vs analyst
Core security design principles (least privilege, defense in depth, fail-safe defaults)
SABSA and TOGAF architecture frameworks overview
CIA triad and beyond in architecture decisions

resources.label: Security Architecture: Design, Deployment and Operations

security-architect.challenge: Draw an annotated architecture diagram for a fictional three-tier web application, labeling all trust boundaries, data flows, and applicable security controls.

security-architect.title: Threat Modeling
security-architect.description: Apply structured threat modeling methodologies to identify, prioritize, and mitigate threats during the design phase rather than after deployment. Practice building Data Flow Diagrams and using STRIDE to enumerate threats systematically.
topics: Threat modeling process and when to apply it
security-architect.challenge: Perform a complete STRIDE threat model on a fictional payment API, producing a DFD, a STRIDE table with 15+ threats, and a prioritized mitigation backlog.

security-architect.title: Identity and Access Management Architecture
security-architect.description: Design identity and access management systems that enforce least-privilege access across users, services, and machines. Understand federation, SSO, and modern identity protocols that underpin enterprise and cloud environments.
topics: Machine identity and service account governance
Directory services (LDAP, Active Directory, Azure AD)

resources.label: Solving Identity Management in Modern Applications

security-architect.challenge: Design a full IAM architecture for a multi-tenant SaaS platform including SSO, MFA, RBAC, PAM, and machine identities with a trust model diagram.

security-architect.title: Network Security Architecture
security-architect.description: Design resilient network security architectures that isolate sensitive assets, control traffic flows, and limit lateral movement. Apply segmentation, demilitarized zones, and encrypted tunnels to real enterprise topologies.
topics: Firewall architecture and rule design principles
East-west traffic inspection and lateral movement prevention

resources.label: NIST SP 800-125B Secure Virtual Network Config

security-architect.challenge: Design a secure network architecture for a retail company with POS, e-commerce, corporate, and cloud segments, including firewall policy and segmentation rationale.

security-architect.title: Cloud Security Architecture
security-architect.description: Architect secure cloud environments using native security services and well-architected framework principles across AWS and Azure. Apply cloud security posture management and enforce guardrails through Infrastructure as Code.
topics: AWS Security Hub and Azure Security Center
Identity and access in cloud (roles, policies, managed identity)
Cloud network security (VPC, security groups, NSGs)
Encryption at rest and in transit in cloud
Landing zone and security baseline design
security-architect.challenge: Design a secure AWS landing zone for a financial services company including account structure, SCPs, guardrails, logging, and network topology.

security-architect.title: Zero Trust Architecture
security-architect.description: Design and implement Zero Trust architectures that eliminate implicit trust for users, devices, and network location. Apply the NIST ZTA model and understand modern ZTNA products replacing legacy VPN models.
topics: Zero Trust principles (verify explicitly, least privilege, assume breach)
Migration path from legacy to Zero Trust
security-architect.challenge: Produce a Zero Trust migration plan for a hybrid enterprise, mapping the current state, target state architecture, and phased implementation roadmap.

security-architect.title: Application Security Architecture
security-architect.description: Embed security into application design by applying OWASP principles, defining security requirements, and selecting appropriate security controls for web, mobile, and API surfaces. Lead security reviews of application designs.
topics: API security design (OAuth scopes, rate limiting, input validation)
Session management and CSRF protection design
Third-party library and supply chain risk
security-architect.challenge: Conduct a security architecture review of a fictional microservices application, producing findings, design recommendations, and a ranked risk register.

security-architect.title: Cryptography in Architecture
security-architect.description: Select and implement the right cryptographic mechanisms for data protection, integrity, and non-repudiation across complex system designs. Understand PKI, key lifecycle management, and emerging post-quantum considerations.
topics: Symmetric vs asymmetric cryptography in system design
PKI design, CA hierarchy, and certificate lifecycle
Key management systems and HSM integration
Encryption at rest patterns (application vs database vs disk level)
security-architect.challenge: Design a PKI and key management architecture for an enterprise, covering CA hierarchy, certificate issuance workflows, HSM integration, and key rotation policy.

security-architect.title: Security Architecture Review and Governance
security-architect.description: Establish and run a Security Architecture Review process that evaluates designs at scale without becoming a bottleneck to development teams. Build a library of approved patterns and a risk acceptance framework for exceptions.
topics: Architecture review board (ARB) setup and process
Tiered review process based on risk level
Integrating architecture review into agile delivery
Security champion programs and developer enablement

resources.label: Building Secure and Reliable Systems (Google SRE)

security-architect.challenge: Design a tiered security architecture review program for a 500-engineer organization, including intake criteria, review SLAs, pattern library structure, and exception tracking.

security-architect.title: Enterprise Security Architecture Leadership
security-architect.description: Lead an enterprise security architecture function that aligns security investments with business strategy and drives multi-year architecture transformation programs. Communicate architecture risk and vision to C-suite and board audiences.
topics: Aligning security architecture to business strategy
Building and communicating a multi-year security roadmap
Managing a team of security architects
Influencing without authority across engineering and product
security-architect.challenge: Present a 3-year enterprise security architecture transformation plan to a simulated board, covering current state risks, target architecture, investment roadmap, and success metrics.

threat-hunter.title: Threat Hunting Foundations
threat-hunter.description: Understand what threat hunting is, how it differs from reactive detection, and what a structured hunting program looks like. Build the analytical mindset required to generate and test hypotheses about adversary behavior.
topics: Threat hunting vs incident response vs detection engineering
Log source fundamentals (Windows events, network logs)
threat-hunter.challenge: Write five hunting hypotheses for a corporate Windows environment, each linked to a specific MITRE ATT&CK technique and listing the data sources required.

threat-hunter.title: MITRE ATT&CK Framework for Hunters
threat-hunter.description: Master the MITRE ATT&CK framework as the primary language for describing, hunting, and detecting adversary techniques. Use ATT&CK Navigator to visualize coverage gaps and prioritize hunting campaigns.
topics: Using ATT&CK Navigator for coverage mapping
Top techniques used by tracked threat groups
threat-hunter.challenge: Use ATT&CK Navigator to visualize your current SOC detection coverage and identify the top five uncovered techniques most commonly used by APT groups targeting your sector.

threat-hunter.title: Windows Event Log Analysis
threat-hunter.description: Develop expertise in Windows telemetry by learning which event IDs expose adversary activity and how to tune logging to catch attacker techniques. Deploy Sysmon for deep process, network, and file telemetry.
topics: Critical Windows Event IDs for security (4624, 4688, 7045, etc.)
PowerShell Script Block logging and transcription
Command-line auditing and process creation logging
Detecting lateral movement via Windows events

resources.label: Detecting Lateral Movement with Windows Events

threat-hunter.challenge: Configure a Sysmon deployment on a test Windows host, generate 10 ATT&CK technique artifacts, and confirm each one is captured in the event log with the expected event IDs.

threat-hunter.title: SIEM Hunting with Splunk or Elastic
threat-hunter.description: Write effective hunt queries in Splunk SPL or Elastic KQL to surface anomalous patterns across large log datasets. Build hunt dashboards that help analysts rapidly triage suspicious activity.
topics: SPL and KQL fundamentals for security use cases
Searching, filtering, and aggregating security events
Statistical hunting techniques (rare, outlier, baseline deviation)
Building hunt dashboards and saved searches
Alert threshold tuning to reduce false positives
threat-hunter.challenge: Write 10 hunt queries in SPL or KQL covering techniques from Initial Access to Exfiltration, each with a comment explaining the detection logic and expected false positive sources.

threat-hunter.title: Network Traffic Analysis for Hunting
threat-hunter.description: Analyze network traffic to detect command-and-control beaconing, data exfiltration, lateral movement, and protocol abuse. Use Zeek to generate structured network metadata at scale for hunt queries.
topics: Network baseline establishment and anomaly detection
DNS query analysis for tunneling and DGA detection
Zeek log schema and query patterns
Detecting data exfiltration via network telemetry

resources.label: The Practice of Network Security Monitoring

threat-hunter.challenge: Analyze a provided packet capture containing C2 traffic, identify the beacon interval, exfiltration method, and at least three network indicators with supporting evidence.

threat-hunter.title: Endpoint Detection and Response (EDR) Hunting
threat-hunter.description: Leverage EDR platforms to hunt for adversary behavior across endpoints using rich process, file, and registry telemetry unavailable in traditional SIEMs. Understand process tree analysis and timeline reconstruction.
topics: EDR telemetry sources and query languages
Process tree analysis for suspicious chains
Hunting with CrowdStrike Falcon or MDE Advanced Hunting
EDR alert triage and investigation workflow

resources.label: Microsoft Defender for Endpoint Advanced Hunting Docs

threat-hunter.challenge: Use MDE Advanced Hunting or CrowdStrike to hunt for suspicious PowerShell spawning from Office processes across a simulated environment, documenting all findings.

threat-hunter.title: Threat Intelligence Integration
threat-hunter.description: Integrate threat intelligence feeds and platforms into the hunt workflow to focus hunts on relevant adversary infrastructure and TTPs. Learn to evaluate, enrich, and operationalize intelligence for proactive detection.
topics: Types of threat intelligence (strategic, tactical, operational)
Consuming threat feeds in MISP and OpenCTI
Enriching alerts and hunt pivots with CTI
Tracking threat actor groups and campaigns
Producing actionable intelligence from hunt findings
threat-hunter.challenge: Ingest a threat feed into MISP, extract 20 IOCs, write hunt queries for each IOC type, and build a MITRE ATT&CK heatmap of the TTPs covered.

threat-hunter.title: Detection Engineering and Sigma Rules
threat-hunter.description: Convert hunt findings into durable, portable detection rules using the Sigma format that can be deployed across multiple SIEM backends. Build a detection lifecycle that tracks rules from hypothesis through retirement.
topics: Converting hunt queries to Sigma rules
Sigma backends for Splunk, Elastic, and QRadar
Detection as Code: versioning and CI/CD for rules
Alert fatigue management and rule quality metrics
SOAR playbook integration for automated triage
threat-hunter.challenge: Write 10 production-quality Sigma rules covering high-priority ATT&CK techniques, validate them with sigma-cli, convert to SPL, and submit a PR to a test rule repository.

threat-hunter.title: Advanced Adversary Simulation and Purple Teaming
threat-hunter.description: Run controlled adversary simulations using Atomic Red Team and MITRE Caldera to validate detection coverage and identify blind spots. Conduct purple team exercises that directly improve hunt and detection capabilities.
topics: Atomic Red Team test execution and logging
Purple team exercise planning and scope
Mapping simulation results to detection gaps
Iterating detection rules based on simulation output
Reporting purple team findings to stakeholders
threat-hunter.challenge: Execute a 20-technique adversary emulation using Atomic Red Team against a test environment, measure detection rate, and produce a gap remediation plan with new Sigma rules.

threat-hunter.title: Hunt Program Operations and Leadership
threat-hunter.description: Build and operate a mature threat hunting program with defined processes, metrics, and continuous improvement cycles integrated into the broader SOC. Lead a team of hunters and communicate program value to leadership.
topics: Hunt program framework and maturity model
KPIs: dwell time, hypothesis validation rate, detection uplift
Cross-team collaboration with IR and detection engineering
Communicating hunt findings to executive stakeholders

resources.label: SOC Analyst to Threat Hunter Career Path

threat-hunter.challenge: Design a quarterly threat hunting program calendar with 6 targeted hunt campaigns, each with a hypothesis, required data sources, success criteria, and output deliverables.

forensics-analyst.title: Digital Forensics Fundamentals
forensics-analyst.description: Establish the principles and legal context of digital forensics including evidence integrity, chain of custody, and courtroom admissibility standards. Understand the investigative process from first response through reporting.
topics: Chain of custody documentation and evidence handling
Types of digital evidence (disk, memory, network, cloud)

resources.label: Digital Forensics with Open Source Tools
NIST SP 800-86 Guide to Integrating Forensic Techniques

forensics-analyst.challenge: Document a mock evidence acquisition of a USB drive including chain of custody form, hash verification steps, and a written procedure for maintaining forensic integrity.

forensics-analyst.title: Disk Imaging and Acquisition
forensics-analyst.description: Perform forensically sound disk and drive acquisitions using hardware write blockers and trusted imaging tools that preserve original evidence. Verify image integrity with cryptographic hashing.
topics: Acquiring with FTK Imager and Guymager
Acquiring from SSDs, encrypted drives, and mobile devices
forensics-analyst.challenge: Image a test USB drive using both FTK Imager and dd, verify hashes match for both images, and document the acquisition process in a formal evidence log.

forensics-analyst.title: File System Forensics
forensics-analyst.description: Analyze file system structures to recover deleted files, examine metadata, and identify timeline artifacts left by user and attacker activity. Use Autopsy and The Sleuth Kit to navigate and carve file system data.
topics: File metadata and MAC times (Modified, Accessed, Created)
Autopsy case setup and evidence analysis
Timeline creation from file system metadata
forensics-analyst.challenge: Analyze a forensic image containing simulated user activity, recover five deleted files, build an activity timeline, and write an analysis report with supporting evidence.

forensics-analyst.title: Windows Artifact Analysis
forensics-analyst.description: Extract and interpret the rich set of forensic artifacts that Windows leaves behind to reconstruct user actions, program execution, and attacker activity. Analyze registry hives, prefetch files, jump lists, and event logs.
topics: Windows Registry forensic artifacts (UserAssist, RecentDocs, MRU)
Prefetch file analysis for program execution evidence
LNK file and jump list analysis
Shellbag analysis for folder access history
Recycle Bin and Volume Shadow Copy analysis
forensics-analyst.challenge: Analyze a Windows disk image from a simulated insider threat case, identify all evidence of data exfiltration activity using at least five distinct artifact types.

forensics-analyst.title: Memory Forensics with Volatility
forensics-analyst.description: Capture and analyze volatile memory to uncover running malware, injected code, network connections, and credentials that leave no disk trace. Master Volatility 3 plugins for process, network, and malware artifact extraction.
topics: Memory acquisition methods and tools (WinPmem, LiME)
Process list and process tree analysis (pslist, pstree)
Extracting registry hives and credentials from memory
forensics-analyst.challenge: Analyze a memory image from a compromised Windows system, identify the malicious process, extract its network indicators, and recover any injected shellcode.

forensics-analyst.title: Network Forensics
forensics-analyst.description: Reconstruct network-based attacker activity from packet captures and flow data to establish timelines of intrusion, data theft, and lateral movement. Carve transferred files and credentials from PCAP evidence.
topics: Full packet capture collection and preservation
Identifying C2 and exfiltration patterns in PCAP
Zeek log analysis for network forensics

resources.label: Network Forensics: Tracking Hackers Through Cyberspace

forensics-analyst.challenge: Analyze a PCAP file from a simulated breach, reconstruct the attacker kill chain from initial access through data exfiltration, carve exfiltrated files, and document findings.

forensics-analyst.title: Mobile and Cloud Forensics
forensics-analyst.description: Extend forensic investigation skills to mobile devices and cloud platforms where modern attackers increasingly operate. Understand logical and physical acquisition of iOS/Android devices and forensic analysis of cloud audit logs.
topics: iOS and Android acquisition methods (logical, filesystem, physical)
Mobile artifact categories (call logs, messages, app data, location)
AWS CloudTrail log analysis for intrusion evidence
forensics-analyst.challenge: Investigate a simulated cloud breach using AWS CloudTrail logs, reconstruct the attacker timeline from initial credential compromise through data exfiltration.

forensics-analyst.title: Malware Forensics and Anti-Forensics Detection
forensics-analyst.description: Identify and counter techniques attackers use to destroy or manipulate forensic evidence including log clearing, timestamp tampering, and data hiding. Detect malware presence through artifact analysis even when the binary is removed.
topics: Detecting log clearing events and tampered logs
Timestamp analysis and MACE manipulation detection
Detecting evidence of file wiping tools
Identifying malware through persistence artifacts without the binary
forensics-analyst.challenge: Investigate a forensic image where an attacker attempted evidence destruction, identify all artifacts indicating log clearing and timestamp manipulation, and document the evasion techniques used.

forensics-analyst.title: Forensic Reporting and Expert Testimony
forensics-analyst.description: Produce legally defensible forensic reports that clearly communicate findings to non-technical attorneys, law enforcement, and executives. Prepare for and survive cross-examination as an expert witness.
topics: Technical report structure for forensic cases
Writing findings for legal vs executive audiences

resources.label: Writing Information Security Policies and Reports

forensics-analyst.challenge: Write a complete forensic investigation report for a simulated insider threat case suitable for court submission, including executive summary, methodology, findings, and conclusions.

forensics-analyst.title: Enterprise DFIR Operations
forensics-analyst.description: Lead digital forensics and incident response operations at enterprise scale using remote forensic tools that enable simultaneous investigation across thousands of endpoints. Build and manage a DFIR program with defined playbooks and SLAs.
topics: Remote forensic collection at scale with Velociraptor
Building forensic triage workflows for SOC escalations
DFIR playbook development for common scenarios
DFIR program metrics and SLA tracking
Cross-functional coordination with legal, HR, and law enforcement
Threat intel integration into DFIR operations

resources.label: The Practice of Network Security Monitoring

forensics-analyst.challenge: Deploy Velociraptor in a lab, build a triage artifact collection pack for ransomware incidents, execute it across five hosts, and produce a consolidated forensic report.

bug-bounty-hunter.title: Bug Bounty Foundations and Ethics
bug-bounty-hunter.description: Understand the legal, ethical, and operational framework of authorized bug bounty hunting before touching any target. Learn how programs define scope, safe harbor protections, and responsible disclosure standards.
topics: What bug bounty programs are and how they work
Reading and respecting program scope and rules
Safe harbor and legal protections for hunters
Setting up HackerOne and Bugcrowd profiles
Common report writing expectations and quality standards
bug-bounty-hunter.challenge: Read three real-world disclosed HackerOne reports, analyze the bug class, impact, and report quality for each, and write a critique identifying what made each report strong or weak.

bug-bounty-hunter.title: Reconnaissance and Attack Surface Mapping
bug-bounty-hunter.description: Build a systematic recon methodology to map the full attack surface of a target before attempting any exploitation. Discover subdomains, open ports, exposed services, and technology fingerprints within authorized scope.
topics: Subdomain enumeration with Amass and Subfinder
Port and service scanning within authorized scope
Technology fingerprinting with Wappalyzer and Whatweb
Google Dorks and search engine recon
Shodan and Censys for asset discovery

resources.label: OWASP Testing Guide v4 - Information Gathering

bug-bounty-hunter.challenge: Perform authorized recon against a HackerOne public program target (within scope), document all discovered assets, technologies, and potential entry points in a structured spreadsheet.

bug-bounty-hunter.title: Web Vulnerability Fundamentals with Burp Suite
bug-bounty-hunter.description: Master Burp Suite as the primary tool for intercepting, modifying, and replaying HTTP traffic to test web application security controls. Learn the core workflow for manually testing web vulnerabilities in authorized programs.
topics: Burp Suite proxy setup and browser configuration
Using Repeater for manual vulnerability testing
Sitemap and crawling within authorized scope
Intruder for brute force and parameter fuzzing
Saving and organizing Burp project files
bug-bounty-hunter.challenge: Complete 10 Web Security Academy labs using Burp Suite covering at least five different vulnerability classes, documenting the exploitation steps and remediation for each.

bug-bounty-hunter.title: OWASP Top 10 in Practice
bug-bounty-hunter.description: Gain hands-on experience exploiting every OWASP Top 10 vulnerability category in legal practice environments before hunting in real authorized programs. Build muscle memory for identifying these issues during recon and testing.
topics: Security misconfiguration and exposed admin panels
Cryptographic failures and sensitive data exposure
bug-bounty-hunter.challenge: Exploit all 10 OWASP Top 10 categories in OWASP Juice Shop, screenshot each finding, and write a proof-of-concept report for each as if submitting to a real bug bounty program.

bug-bounty-hunter.title: Authentication and Authorization Bugs
bug-bounty-hunter.description: Develop expertise in finding authentication flaws and authorization bypasses - consistently among the highest-paid vulnerability classes in bug bounty programs. Learn to systematically test IDOR, BOLA, and broken OAuth implementations.
topics: IDOR discovery methodology and horizontal vs vertical privilege escalation
Testing multi-step workflows for authorization gaps
OAuth 2.0 common vulnerabilities (open redirect, state bypass, token leakage)
JWT algorithm confusion and signature bypass
bug-bounty-hunter.challenge: Find and responsibly report an IDOR or authorization bypass in a live authorized bug bounty program, submitting a high-quality report with impact analysis and reproduction steps.

bug-bounty-hunter.title: Fuzzing and Automated Vulnerability Discovery
bug-bounty-hunter.description: Automate vulnerability discovery at scale using fuzzing and template-based scanning to find issues across large scopes faster than manual testing alone. Learn to build custom Nuclei templates and ffuf wordlists for specific targets.
topics: Directory and parameter fuzzing with ffuf
Nuclei template-based scanning within authorized scope
Building custom wordlists from target technology
Interpreting and triaging automated scan results
bug-bounty-hunter.challenge: Build a custom recon and fuzzing pipeline for an authorized target, write three custom Nuclei templates for the target technology stack, and document all validated findings.

bug-bounty-hunter.title: Server-Side Vulnerabilities
bug-bounty-hunter.description: Master server-side vulnerability classes that often lead to critical and high severity findings with major business impact in authorized programs. Learn to identify and exploit SSRF, XXE, and insecure deserialization issues.
topics: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) identification and exploitation
Cloud metadata endpoint abuse via SSRF
Insecure deserialization identification and gadget chains
Path traversal and local file inclusion
bug-bounty-hunter.challenge: Complete all SSRF, XXE, and SSTI labs on PortSwigger Web Security Academy and find one server-side vulnerability in an authorized program to report.

bug-bounty-hunter.title: API Security Testing
bug-bounty-hunter.description: Test REST and GraphQL APIs for security issues using the OWASP API Security Top 10 as a guide, with specialized tooling for API-specific attack classes. Many modern programs have large API attack surfaces with fewer eyes on them.
topics: REST API testing methodology with Burp and Postman
Excessive data exposure in API responses
API rate limiting and resource exhaustion testing
API key leakage and authentication bypass
bug-bounty-hunter.challenge: Perform a full API security assessment against a vulnerable API lab (crAPI or vAPI), exploiting at least 8 of the OWASP API Security Top 10 categories and writing a professional report.

bug-bounty-hunter.title: Business Logic and High-Impact Chaining
bug-bounty-hunter.description: Find and exploit business logic vulnerabilities that automated scanners miss entirely, and chain multiple low-severity bugs into high or critical severity findings through creative impact analysis. Learn to maximize bounty rewards by demonstrating full impact.
topics: Common logic flaws (price manipulation, workflow bypass, race conditions)
Chaining low-severity bugs for maximum impact
Escalating SSRF to RCE or credential theft
Writing PoC code that demonstrates real-world impact
Impact justification in reports to increase bounty value
bug-bounty-hunter.challenge: Find a business logic vulnerability or vulnerability chain in an authorized program that results in a high or critical severity finding, and submit a report with complete impact demonstration.

bug-bounty-hunter.title: Advanced Techniques and Program Scaling
bug-bounty-hunter.description: Operate as a professional bug bounty hunter with systematic workflows, custom automation, and a strong reputation on major platforms. Build sustainable hunting processes that scale across multiple programs and bug classes.
topics: Building a personal bug bounty automation pipeline
Continuous monitoring for new assets and changes
Writing top-tier reports that maximize bounty rewards
Building platform reputation and private program access
Collaborating with other hunters on team bounties
Tax, legal, and business considerations for full-time hunters
bug-bounty-hunter.challenge: Build a fully automated recon pipeline that monitors three authorized program scopes for new assets, runs Nuclei scans on discovered targets, and sends alerts for new findings.

pentester.title: Networking and OS Fundamentals
pentester.description: Build a solid foundation in networking protocols and operating system internals. Understanding how systems communicate is essential before attempting any security testing.
pentester.challenge: Capture and analyze HTTP and DNS traffic on a local network using Wireshark.

pentester.title: Security Concepts and Ethics
pentester.description: Learn core security concepts, vulnerability scoring, and the legal boundaries of ethical hacking. All testing must occur only in authorized lab environments.
pentester.challenge: Research three real CVEs and summarize their CVSS scores and impact.

pentester.title: Kali Linux and Lab Setup
pentester.description: Set up a personal penetration testing lab using Kali Linux and virtualization tools. Practice all techniques exclusively inside isolated lab environments.
pentester.challenge: Deploy Metasploitable2 in VirtualBox and run a full Nmap scan against it.

pentester.title: Reconnaissance and OSINT
pentester.description: Learn passive and active reconnaissance techniques to gather target intelligence before an engagement. Use only authorized targets and public information sources.
pentester.challenge: Perform full passive and active recon against a personal authorized lab VM.

pentester.title: Vulnerability Scanning
pentester.description: Use automated scanning tools to identify known vulnerabilities in target systems. Always obtain written authorization before scanning any system.
pentester.challenge: Run Nessus and Nikto against a lab web server and produce a findings report.

pentester.title: Exploitation with Metasploit
pentester.description: Learn to use Metasploit Framework to exploit known vulnerabilities in lab environments. Use Metasploit only against machines you own or have explicit written permission to test.
pentester.challenge: Exploit a known vulnerability on Metasploitable2 and gain a Meterpreter shell.

pentester.title: Web Application Pentesting
pentester.description: Learn to identify and exploit common web vulnerabilities including SQL injection, XSS, and CSRF. Practice exclusively on DVWA, OWASP WebGoat, or authorized web targets.
pentester.challenge: Complete 10 Web Security Academy labs covering SQL injection and XSS.

pentester.title: Password Attacks and Privilege Escalation
pentester.description: Learn password cracking techniques and privilege escalation paths on Linux and Windows systems. Practice only in isolated authorized lab environments.
pentester.challenge: Crack a set of MD5 and bcrypt hashes using Hashcat with a wordlist.

pentester.title: Reporting and Pentest Methodology
pentester.description: Learn to document findings professionally and follow standard penetration testing methodologies. A well-written report is as important as the technical findings themselves.
pentester.challenge: Write a complete pentest report for your Metasploitable2 lab engagement.

pentester.title: Advanced Exploitation and OSCP Prep
pentester.description: Master advanced exploitation including buffer overflows and Active Directory attacks in authorized lab environments. This layer prepares you for OSCP-level certification challenges.
pentester.challenge: Root five HackTheBox machines independently and document each exploitation chain.

soc-analyst.title: IT and Networking Foundations
soc-analyst.description: Establish core networking and IT knowledge required for monitoring and incident analysis. SOC analysts must understand normal traffic to identify anomalies.
soc-analyst.challenge: Identify all DNS queries and HTTP requests in a provided PCAP file.

soc-analyst.title: Security Fundamentals
soc-analyst.description: Learn foundational security concepts and threat frameworks used daily in SOC operations. Familiarity with MITRE ATT&CK accelerates alert triage and investigation.
soc-analyst.challenge: Map a known malware campaign to at least five MITRE ATT&CK techniques.

soc-analyst.title: Log Analysis
soc-analyst.description: Develop skills in reading and parsing logs from operating systems and applications. Log analysis is the primary daily activity for Tier 1 and Tier 2 SOC analysts.
topics: Log parsing with grep and awk
soc-analyst.challenge: Identify a brute-force attack in a Windows Security event log export.

soc-analyst.title: SIEM Fundamentals
soc-analyst.description: Learn to ingest, search, and correlate security events using a SIEM platform. Splunk and Elastic SIEM are the most common platforms in enterprise SOC environments.
soc-analyst.challenge: Build a Splunk dashboard showing failed logins and geographic source IPs.

soc-analyst.title: IDS and IPS
soc-analyst.description: Understand how intrusion detection and prevention systems generate and classify alerts. Analysts must tune IDS rules to minimize false positives while catching real threats.
soc-analyst.challenge: Write a custom Snort rule to detect ICMP flood traffic in a lab environment.

soc-analyst.title: Threat Intelligence
soc-analyst.description: Learn to consume and apply threat intelligence to improve detection and investigation quality. Threat intel enriches alerts with context about known adversaries and indicators.
soc-analyst.challenge: Analyze a malware sample hash in VirusTotal and map findings to ATT&CK.

soc-analyst.title: Incident Triage and Response
soc-analyst.description: Learn structured incident triage workflows and how to escalate and document security events. Following playbooks ensures consistent and thorough response to common alert types.
soc-analyst.challenge: Triage five simulated alerts on LetsDefend and document your analysis for each.

soc-analyst.title: Malware Analysis Basics
soc-analyst.description: Develop basic malware analysis skills to enrich SOC investigations without a dedicated reverse engineering role. Static and dynamic analysis helps classify threats quickly.
soc-analyst.challenge: Submit a malware sample to Any.run and produce a behavioral analysis report.

soc-analyst.title: Advanced SIEM and Detection Engineering
soc-analyst.description: Build custom detection rules and advanced queries to proactively find threats in SIEM data. Detection engineering reduces analyst fatigue by automating high-confidence alert logic.
soc-analyst.challenge: Write a Sigma rule detecting lateral movement via PsExec and test it in Splunk.

soc-analyst.title: Threat Hunting and SOC Maturity
soc-analyst.description: Move from reactive alert-handling to proactive threat hunting using hypothesis-driven methodologies. Senior SOC analysts lead hunting initiatives that surface threats before they trigger alerts.
soc-analyst.challenge: Conduct a hypothesis-based hunt for persistence mechanisms in a lab SIEM dataset.

appsec-engineer.title: Secure Development Foundations
appsec-engineer.description: Understand web application architecture and how developers build modern applications. AppSec engineers must read and reason about code to find and fix security issues.
appsec-engineer.challenge: Document the full authentication flow of an open-source web application on GitHub.

appsec-engineer.title: OWASP Top 10
appsec-engineer.description: Study the OWASP Top 10 vulnerabilities and understand how each occurs and how to remediate them. Hands-on practice with intentionally vulnerable apps reinforces each concept.
appsec-engineer.challenge: Exploit and then remediate all OWASP Top 10 categories in DVWA.

appsec-engineer.title: Static Analysis and SAST
appsec-engineer.description: Learn to use static application security testing tools to find vulnerabilities in source code. SAST shifts security left by catching issues before code reaches production.
appsec-engineer.challenge: Run Semgrep against an open-source Python app and triage every finding.

appsec-engineer.title: Dynamic Analysis and DAST
appsec-engineer.description: Use dynamic testing tools to find runtime vulnerabilities in running applications. DAST complements SAST by catching issues that only appear during execution.
appsec-engineer.challenge: Run a full OWASP ZAP active scan against DVWA and document all findings.

appsec-engineer.title: Threat Modeling
appsec-engineer.description: Learn structured threat modeling to identify security risks during the design phase. Early threat modeling prevents costly redesigns and reduces attack surface before code is written.
appsec-engineer.challenge: Create a complete STRIDE threat model for a simple login and session management flow.

appsec-engineer.title: Secure Code Review
appsec-engineer.description: Develop the ability to manually review source code for security vulnerabilities across common languages. Manual review catches logic flaws that automated tools miss.
appsec-engineer.challenge: Review a provided Python Flask app and identify all input validation and auth issues.

appsec-engineer.title: API Security
appsec-engineer.description: Learn the unique security challenges of modern REST and GraphQL APIs. APIs are a primary attack surface in modern applications and require dedicated security testing approaches.
appsec-engineer.challenge: Exploit BOLA and JWT signature bypass vulnerabilities in a lab API.

appsec-engineer.title: DevSecOps and CI/CD Security
appsec-engineer.description: Integrate security tooling into CI/CD pipelines so security checks run on every code change. DevSecOps embeds AppSec engineers as partners in the development workflow.
appsec-engineer.challenge: Configure a GitHub Actions workflow that runs Semgrep and blocks merges on critical findings.

appsec-engineer.title: Vulnerability Management and Triage
appsec-engineer.description: Learn to prioritize and manage a vulnerability backlog across engineering teams. AppSec engineers must balance security risk against development velocity with clear communication.
appsec-engineer.challenge: Score and prioritize ten sample AppSec findings using CVSS and present a remediation plan.

appsec-engineer.title: Advanced AppSec Program Leadership
appsec-engineer.description: Lead a mature application security program by building developer security culture and measuring program effectiveness. Senior AppSec engineers drive organizational security improvement through metrics and advocacy.
appsec-engineer.challenge: Assess a sample company against OWASP SAMM and write a 12-month improvement roadmap.

red-team.title: Foundations and Legal Framework
red-team.description: Understand the legal, ethical, and operational framework that governs red team engagements. All red team activity must occur only against authorized targets under a signed rules of engagement.
red-team.challenge: Draft a rules of engagement document for a simulated red team engagement scenario.

red-team.title: Pentesting Prerequisites
red-team.description: Ensure solid penetration testing fundamentals before progressing to red team tradecraft. Red teamers build on pentesting skills with added emphasis on stealth and persistence.
red-team.challenge: Complete HackTheBox Starting Point Tier 2 machines independently.

red-team.title: OPSEC and Tradecraft
red-team.description: Learn operational security practices that prevent detection during authorized red team engagements. Good OPSEC simulates real adversary behavior and tests blue team detection capabilities.
red-team.challenge: Set up a basic redirector infrastructure in an authorized lab using a VPS and test C2 connectivity.

red-team.title: Command and Control
red-team.description: Learn to operate C2 frameworks to manage implants and execute post-exploitation tasks during authorized engagements. C2 infrastructure forms the backbone of red team operations.
red-team.challenge: Deploy Sliver C2 in an authorized lab, create a beacon, and execute post-exploitation commands.

red-team.title: Active Directory Attacks
red-team.description: Master Active Directory attack techniques including enumeration, privilege escalation, and lateral movement in authorized lab environments. AD attacks are central to most enterprise red team engagements.
red-team.challenge: Compromise a lab AD domain from initial access to domain admin using BloodHound paths.

red-team.title: Phishing and Initial Access
red-team.description: Learn social engineering and phishing techniques used to simulate real-world initial access in authorized engagements. Phishing remains the most common initial access vector in enterprise breaches.
red-team.challenge: Run a GoPhish campaign against a lab environment and capture credentials via a login page clone.

red-team.title: Evasion and AV Bypass
red-team.description: Learn payload obfuscation and AV/EDR evasion techniques for authorized lab use. Bypassing security controls tests the real-world effectiveness of a target organizations defenses.
red-team.challenge: Create a shellcode loader that bypasses Windows Defender in an authorized lab VM.

red-team.title: Lateral Movement and Persistence
red-team.description: Execute lateral movement and establish persistence mechanisms as part of authorized adversary simulation. These techniques test whether blue teams can detect long-dwell threats.
red-team.challenge: Establish three different persistence mechanisms on a lab Windows host and verify survivability across reboots.

red-team.title: Reporting and Adversary Emulation
red-team.description: Document red team findings with precision and map techniques to ATT&CK for blue team improvement. Red team reports must communicate risk clearly to both executive and technical audiences.
red-team.challenge: Write a full red team report for a lab engagement including executive summary and ATT&CK mapping.

red-team.title: Advanced Adversary Simulation
red-team.description: Simulate advanced persistent threat actor TTPs in authorized purple team and TIBER-style engagements. Elite red teamers collaborate directly with blue teams to rapidly improve enterprise defenses.
red-team.challenge: Design and execute a purple team exercise emulating a named APT group against an authorized lab environment.

blue-team.title: Networking and OS Security Basics
blue-team.description: Build the foundational networking and operating system knowledge needed for defensive security work. Blue teamers must deeply understand normal system behavior to detect deviations.
blue-team.challenge: Harden a fresh Windows 10 VM using the CIS Benchmark Level 1 checklist.

blue-team.title: Hardening and Secure Configuration
blue-team.description: Apply industry-standard hardening baselines to operating systems and applications. Reducing attack surface through hardening is the most cost-effective defensive control.
blue-team.challenge: Run Lynis against a Linux VM and remediate all high-severity findings.

blue-team.title: Log Analysis and Monitoring
blue-team.description: Collect and analyze logs from endpoints, servers, and network devices to detect threats. Centralized logging is the foundation of any effective defensive monitoring capability.
blue-team.challenge: Deploy Elastic SIEM in a lab and ingest Windows and Linux endpoint logs.

blue-team.title: EDR and Endpoint Security
blue-team.description: Deploy and configure endpoint detection and response tools to monitor and protect endpoints. EDR provides deep visibility into endpoint activity that traditional AV cannot match.
blue-team.challenge: Deploy Sysmon with the SwiftOnSecurity config and detect a Mimikatz execution.

blue-team.title: Network Defense
blue-team.description: Monitor and protect network infrastructure using network security tools and traffic analysis. Network-based controls complement endpoint defenses and catch threats that bypass EDR.
blue-team.challenge: Configure Suricata to detect port scans and alert on them in a lab network.

blue-team.title: Incident Response
blue-team.description: Execute structured incident response procedures to contain and eradicate threats quickly. A well-rehearsed IR process minimizes attacker dwell time and business impact.
blue-team.challenge: Respond to a simulated ransomware incident in a lab, document all IR steps, and write a post-incident report.

blue-team.title: Digital Forensics
blue-team.description: Investigate compromised systems using digital forensics tools to reconstruct attacker activity. Forensic evidence supports legal proceedings and improves detection after an incident.
blue-team.challenge: Analyze a provided memory dump with Volatility and identify all running malicious processes.

blue-team.title: Threat Intelligence Integration
blue-team.description: Ingest and operationalize threat intelligence to proactively block and detect known threats. Integrating intelligence feeds with SIEM and firewalls automates response to known indicators.
blue-team.challenge: Configure MISP to receive a public threat feed and push IOCs into Elastic SIEM alerts.

blue-team.title: Detection Engineering
blue-team.description: Build and maintain high-fidelity detection rules mapped to the MITRE ATT&CK framework. Detection engineering reduces noise and ensures critical attacker techniques reliably trigger alerts.
blue-team.challenge: Write and deploy five Sigma rules covering different ATT&CK techniques in a lab SIEM.

blue-team.title: Blue Team Program Maturity
blue-team.description: Lead a mature defensive security program by measuring effectiveness and continuously improving through purple teaming. Senior blue teamers drive organizational resilience through data-driven decision making.
blue-team.challenge: Design and run a tabletop incident response exercise for a simulated ransomware attack scenario.

cloud-security.title: Cloud Computing Fundamentals
cloud-security.description: Understand cloud service models, deployment types, and the shared responsibility model. Cloud security engineers must know what security obligations fall to the customer versus the provider.
cloud-security.challenge: Create an AWS free-tier account and document the shared responsibility boundaries for EC2 and S3.

cloud-security.title: Identity and Access Management
cloud-security.description: Master cloud IAM concepts including roles, policies, and the principle of least privilege. IAM misconfiguration is the most common cause of cloud security breaches.
cloud-security.challenge: Audit an AWS account IAM configuration and eliminate all overly permissive policies.

cloud-security.title: Network Security in the Cloud
cloud-security.description: Design and implement secure cloud network architectures using VPCs, security groups, and access control lists. Network segmentation in the cloud prevents lateral movement between workloads.
cloud-security.challenge: Design a three-tier VPC architecture with proper security group rules and document each boundary.

cloud-security.title: Cloud Security Posture Management
cloud-security.description: Use CSPM tools to continuously assess cloud environment configurations against security benchmarks. CSPM catches misconfigurations before attackers discover them.
cloud-security.challenge: Run Scout Suite against a lab AWS account and remediate all critical findings.

cloud-security.title: Data Security and Encryption
cloud-security.description: Protect data at rest and in transit using cloud-native encryption and secrets management services. Unencrypted data stores and exposed secrets are leading causes of cloud data breaches.
cloud-security.challenge: Configure S3 server-side encryption with KMS and enforce it via a bucket policy.

cloud-security.title: Logging, Monitoring, and Alerting
cloud-security.description: Implement comprehensive cloud logging and threat detection using native and third-party tools. Without proper logging, cloud incidents go undetected for months.
cloud-security.challenge: Configure GuardDuty and route high-severity findings to an SNS email alert.

cloud-security.title: Zero Trust Architecture
cloud-security.description: Design and implement Zero Trust network access patterns that assume breach at all times. Zero Trust replaces perimeter-based security with identity-centric continuous verification.
cloud-security.challenge: Implement a Zero Trust access pattern for a lab application using AWS Verified Access.

cloud-security.title: Container and Kubernetes Security
cloud-security.description: Secure containerized workloads from image build through runtime using cloud-native tools. Container escape and cluster misconfiguration are critical risks in modern cloud deployments.
cloud-security.challenge: Scan a Kubernetes cluster with kube-bench and remediate all CIS benchmark failures.

cloud-security.title: Cloud Incident Response
cloud-security.description: Execute cloud-specific incident response procedures to investigate and contain cloud security incidents. Cloud IR requires different tooling and skills compared to traditional endpoint-based response.
cloud-security.challenge: Simulate an IAM key compromise in a lab environment and execute a full cloud IR runbook.

cloud-security.title: Cloud Security Architecture and Governance
cloud-security.description: Design enterprise-scale cloud security architectures with multi-account governance and policy automation. Senior cloud security engineers build guardrails that enforce security at scale across hundreds of accounts.
cloud-security.challenge: Design a multi-account AWS landing zone with SCPs enforcing encryption and logging in all child accounts.
